meaning: vt.使能够,授予权利或方法
examples: The new test should enable doctors to detect the disease early.
This will enable the audience to sit in comfort while watching the shows.
The spacecraft will enable scientists to study some dark corners of the solar system.
Until then Hunt is hoping to attract sponsorship to enable her to concentrate on archery full-time.
Until recently, however, the tools to enable differentiated instruction to meet student needs were scarce.
